core_mechanism: Degenerate (spectral) perturbation theory: reducing a small perturbation of a periodic unitary operator that possesses degenerate (non-simple) eigenvalues to an effective Hermitian generator acting on the degenerate eigenspace, which then governs the system's slow asymptotic dynamics
brief_reason: The abstract treats the vector potential as a perturbation of a periodic operator whose response depends on spectral structure, and shows that when the graph has a non-simple (degenerate) eigenvalue a derived Hermitian matrix on that eigenspace characterizes robustness and asymptotically generates the perturbed continuous-time dynamics.
